results.title: Namma Metro Green Line to induct three new six-coach trains by August 2026
results.description: BMRCL plans to introduce new six-coach trainsets on the Green Line to reduce overcrowding, improve train availability and strengthen metro services across Bengaluru. Daily commuters using Bengaluru's Namma Metro can expect less crowding on trains, with the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited (BMRCL) set to introduce three new six-coach trainsets on the Green Line by August 2026. The move is aimed at improving passenger comfort, reducing congestion and increasing train availability on one of the busiest corridors of the city's metro network. The three trainsets are part of a larger fleet being supplied by China Railway Rolling Stock Corporation (CRRC) in partnership with Titagarh Rail Systems Ltd. (TRSL). One of these is the long-awaited prototype train, which arrived in Bengaluru in January 2025 after facing considerable delays. Also Read: 202.7 km of footpaths cleared in just three days as Bengaluru intensifies anti-encroachment drive Although the prototype was originally planned for operation on the Purple Line, BMRCL has now decided to deploy it on the Green Line along with the other newly delivered trainsets. So far, BMRCL has received three Distance-To-Go (DTG)-enabled six-coach trainsets, including the prototype. However, officials said the testing process has taken longer than expected because trial runs can only be conducted during non-revenue hours, when regular passenger services are not operating on the Green Line. Before entering commercial service, each trainset must complete an extensive validation process. This includes multiple performance trials, obtaining 37 statutory safety clearances, and securing approval for the rolling stock before it can carry passengers. BMRCL has decided that all 23 CRRC-Titagarh trainsets will be deployed exclusively on the Green Line. While three trainsets have already been delivered, the remaining 20 trainsets are currently being manufactured at Titagarh Rail Systems' production facility in Uttarpara, West Bengal. Also Read: Gruha Jyothi survey: Sharing caste details is optional, clarifies BESCOM The arrival of the new fleet is also expected to improve services on the Purple Line. Once the new six-coach trains begin operating on the Green Line, the existing Green Line trainsets will be shifted to the Purple Line, which consistently records the highest passenger footfall in the Namma Metro network. According to officials, this redistribution of trains will increase train availability on both corridors and help reduce overcrowding for daily commuters. The new trainsets are equipped with Distance-To-Go (DTG) technology and have a design similar to the Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC)-compatible rolling stock currently being used on the Yellow Line, which connects R.V. Road and Bommasandra. With the induction of the new fleet, BMRCL expects smoother operations, better train distribution and a more comfortable travel experience for passengers using Bengaluru's metro network.

results.title: Vijay Kedia shares a simple market lesson that most retail investors miss: Don't chase today's favourite
results.description: In a post on social media platform X on July 2, Kedia said markets do not move in a straight line but instead rotate through different phases, creating fresh opportunities for investors who can identify them early.

results.title: Proverb of the day: 'Kal kare so aaj kar, aaj kare so ab' - a timeless lesson for the lazy by Sant Kabir and why one should prioritise work
results.description: Sant Kabir's timeless wisdom, urging immediate action over delay, resonates powerfully today amidst digital distractions. His famous doha, "Kal kare so aaj kar, aaj kare so ab," highlights life's uncertainty and the risks of procrastination. This ancient advice, far from promoting haste, champions decisive action, reminding us that the present moment is our only guarantee, not a distant, uncertain tomorrow.

results.title: Heavy to Extremely Heavy Rain Likely Across Odisha; Red Alert for 5 Districts
results.description: #ସ ାରା_ଓଡ଼ିଶାରେ_ପ୍ରବଳ ବର୍ଷିବ ! ଭୋରରୁ ବର୍ଷୁଛି ଯେ ବର୍ଷୁଛି । ଛାଡ଼ିବାର ନାଁ ଧରୁନି । କେଉଁଠି ଅତିପ୍ରବଳ ତ ଆଉ କେଉଁଠି ଅତିଭୀଷଣ । ଭୁବନେଶ୍ବରରୁ ବରଗଡ଼, ମାଲକାନଗିରିରୁ ମୟୂରଭଞ୍ଜ ପର୍ଯ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଜାରି ରହିଛି ଲଗାଣ ବର୍ଷା। ଏହାରି ଭିତରେ ବଙ୍ଗୋପସାଗରରେ ଘନୀଭୂତ ହୋଇ ସ୍ପଷ୍ଟଚିହ୍ନିତ ଲଘୁଚାପ କ୍ଷେତ୍ରରେ ପରିଣତ ହୋଇଛି ଲଘୁଚାପ କ୍ଷେତ୍ର। ଉତ୍ତରପଶ୍ଚିମ ବଙ୍ଗୋପସାଗର ଓ ପାର୍ଶ୍ଵବର୍ତ୍ତୀ ଅଂଚଳରେ ସ୍ପଷ୍ଟଚିହ୍ନିତ ହୋଇଛି। ପ୍ରଭାବରେ ବର୍ଷା ଆହୁରି ବଢିବ ବୋଲି ଆକଳନ କରିଛି ପାଣିପାଗ କେନ୍ଦ୍ର । ଭୀଷଣରୁ ଅତିଭୀଷଣ ବର୍ଷା ନେଇ ୫ ଜିଲ୍ଲା କନ୍ଧମାଳ, ବୌଦ୍ଧ, ଅନୁଗୁଳ, ସମ୍ବଲପୁର ଓ ସୋନପୁରକୁ ଜାରି ହୋଇଛି ରେଡ୍ ଆଲର୍ଟ । ସେହିଭଳି ୮ ଜିଲ୍ଲାରେ ଅତିପ୍ରବଳ ବର୍ଷା ନେଇ ଅରେଞ୍ଜ ୱାର୍ଣ୍ଣିଂ ଜାରି କରାଯାଇଛି । ସେହି ଜିଲ୍ଲା ଗୁଡ଼ିକ ହେଲା କଳାହାଣ୍ଡି, ରାୟଗଡ଼ା, ଗଜପତି, ଗଞ୍ଜାମ, ନୟାଗଡ଼, କଟକ, ଢେଙ୍କାନାଳ ଓ କେନ୍ଦୁଝର । ସେହିଭଳି ବାକି ୧୭ ଜିଲ୍ଲାରେ ପ୍ରବଳ ବର୍ଷା ପାଇଁ ୟେଲୋ ଆଲର୍ଟ ଜାରି କରାଯାଇଛି। ସମୁଦ୍ର ଅଶାନ୍ତ ରହୁଥିବାରୁ ମତ୍ସ୍ୟଜୀବୀଙ୍କୁ ବାରଣ କରାଯାଇଛି । ତେବେ କାଲି ବର୍ଷାରୁ ସାମାନ୍ୟ ଆଶ୍ବସ୍ତି ମିଳିପାରେ । ଆସନ୍ତାକାଲି ଉତ୍ତର ଓ ପଶ୍ଚିମ ଓଡ଼ିଶାରେ ପ୍ରବଳରୁ ଅତିପ୍ରବଳ ବର୍ଷା ହୋଇପାରେ । ଏଥିପାଇଁ ୩ ଜିଲ୍ଲାକୁ ଅରେଞ୍ଜ ଓ ୧୦ ଜିଲ୍ଲାକୁ ୟେଲୋ ଆଲର୍ଟ ଜାରି କରାଯାଇଛି । #OdishaRain #RedAlert #WeatherUpdate #IMD #HeavyRain #Monsoon #OdishaWeather #BreakingNews #OdishaTV #OTV Relentless rainfall has continued across Odisha since early morning, with heavy to extremely heavy showers reported from Bhubaneswar to Bargarh and from Malkangiri to Mayurbhanj. Meanwhile, the low-pressure area over the northwest Bay of Bengal has intensified into a well-marked low-pressure area. According to the weather office, the system is likely to further enhance rainfall activity across the state. A Red Alert has been issued for Kandhamal, Boudh, Angul, Sambalpur and Subarnapur (Sonepur), warning of heavy to extremely heavy rainfall. An Orange Warning has been issued for Kalahandi, Rayagada, Gajapati, Ganjam, Nayagarh, Cuttack, Dhenkanal and Keonjhar, while the remaining 17 districts are under a Yellow Alert for heavy rainfall. Fishermen have been advised not to venture into the sea due to rough sea conditions. The weather office has indicated that rainfall may ease slightly tomorrow. However, north and western Odisha are expected to continue receiving heavy to very heavy rainfall, with Orange Alerts issued for three districts and Yellow Alerts for ten districts.
